name: Combined
description_short: Combined FCU is a member-owned credit union offering checking, savings, auto loans, and certificates of deposit with competitive rates up to 4.20%.
description_long:
Combined Federal Credit Union is a not-for-profit, member-owned financial institution that serves working families and individuals seeking alternatives to traditional banks. The credit union provides a full suite of financial products including share draft checking accounts, share savings accounts, auto loans, and share certificates, positioning itself as an accessible option for everyday banking needs with a stated focus on affordability and personal service.

Combined FCU's core offerings include checking accounts with a low $25 minimum deposit, savings accounts with no limitations on deposits or withdrawals, competitively-rated auto loans, and share certificates with rates advertised up to 4.20%. The credit union also provides mortgage services through partnerships and references CARFAX and "Love My Credit Union" programs on its website, suggesting additional member benefits and services beyond basic deposit and loan products.

The institution differentiates itself through its credit union structure—member ownership rather than shareholder profit maximization—and emphasis on personal service alongside affordable loan payments. The website highlights flexibility in savings products with no withdrawal restrictions and promotes membership accessibility, particularly to working families. The organization maintains an online banking platform and mobile accessibility for members who need on-the-go account access.

While Combined FCU presents itself as a full-service credit union, the website provides limited detail on eligibility requirements, specific loan terms, fee structures, or the complete range of available services. Prospective members would need to contact the institution directly to understand membership eligibility, detailed product terms, and how rates compare to other credit unions in their area. The reliance on third-party links for some services (mortgages) may indicate limited in-house lending capabilities for certain products.
services: Member rates and current offerings information
pros: Low account opening minimum of just $25 for share draft checking accounts
Share savings accounts with no limitations on deposits or withdrawals
Competitive share certificate rates advertised up to 4.20%
Member-owned credit union structure prioritizes member benefits over shareholder profits
Auto loan focus with emphasis on affordable payments and personal service
Mobile banking and online access available for account management
Federal credit union (NCUA-insured) providing deposit protection
cons: Website lacks specific information about membership eligibility requirements or restrictions
No detailed fee schedule or account fee information provided on the site
Limited transparency on actual auto loan rates, APRs, or loan terms
Mortgage services appear to be provided through third-party partnerships rather than in-house
Minimal information about credit union hours, locations, or full range of available services
best_for: Working families seeking low-barrier account opening with minimal deposits
Auto loan borrowers prioritizing affordable rates and personal service
Savers looking for competitive certificate of deposit rates
Members wanting NCUA-insured deposits with credit union member-owner benefits
diagnosis: Combined FCU is best for working families and individuals seeking basic banking services and affordable auto loans through a member-owned credit union structure with low account minimums. The main caveat is that the website provides limited transparency on specific rates, fees, membership eligibility, and detailed product terms, requiring direct contact with the credit union for complete information.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
